skill.name: longbridge-quant
skill.description: Quantitative finance analysis skill for Longbridge data. It guides agents through pairs trading, volatility regimes, seasonality, multi-factor screening, factor research, correlation analysis, statistical tests, optimization, execution modeling, hedging, and machine-learning strategy workflows.
skill.summary: Analyze quantitative trading strategies with Longbridge market data and documented financial frameworks.
skill.author: longbridge

security_audit.summary: Static analysis flagged many markdown backticks, weak-crypto patterns, reconnaissance strings, and Windows SAM strings. Review found the weak-crypto, SAM, and reconnaissance alerts to be false positives from finance terminology and help-command examples, while the documented Longbridge CLI commands are legitimate but real external command execution. No prompt-injection text, credential exfiltration, hidden malware, or destructive behavior was found.

medium_findings.title: External Longbridge CLI Commands in Skill Guidance
medium_findings.description: The skill instructs agents to run Longbridge CLI commands for market data, factor data, options, trades, and analysis setup. These commands are domain-appropriate, but they still execute external programs and may contact Longbridge services, so users should approve commands and verify symbols and flags.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The files explicitly document executable longbridge and pip commands. The usage is legitimate for this financial-data skill, but the external command surface is real.

medium_findings.title: Authenticated Portfolio Data Command Guidance
medium_findings.description: The hedging workflow includes commands for current portfolio positions and notes that trade-scope login may be required. This can expose sensitive portfolio holdings if run without clear user intent, although no exfiltration destination or malicious handling was found.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The command and login requirement are explicit in the hedging reference. Risk depends on user authorization and runtime permissions, so this is medium rather than high.

medium_findings.title: Server-Side User Indicator Script Execution
medium_findings.description: The quant CLI reference describes sending user-defined indicator scripts to Longbridge for server-side execution. This appears to be an intended product feature, but users should avoid submitting secrets or unrelated proprietary code in scripts.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The reference clearly states that scripts execute server-side and can be passed inline or through stdin. It is product functionality, not evidence of malicious behavior.

low_findings.title: False Positive Weak-Crypto Alerts from Finance Notation
low_findings.description: Static weak-cryptography alerts match finance and statistics notation such as log formulas, max drawdown labels, and sample-period labels. No hashing, encryption, or cryptographic algorithm use was found in the reviewed context.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines contain quantitative-finance notation and report labels, not cryptographic calls. No code path uses weak crypto in the inspected files.

low_findings.title: False Positive Windows SAM Alerts from Sample-Period Text
low_findings.description: The critical Windows SAM alerts are caused by the substring in in-sample and out-of-sample report labels. The file does not reference the Windows SAM database, credential extraction, or local system files.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The reviewed lines only show strategy optimization report headings. The SAM match is a substring false positive with no credential context.

low_findings.title: False Positive System-Reconnaissance Alerts from Help Commands
low_findings.description: Static reconnaissance alerts correspond to documented CLI help checks such as running Longbridge subcommands with help flags. These checks discover product command options, not host identity, network configuration, or system inventory.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The commands are scoped to Longbridge CLI usage and flag discovery. No host reconnaissance commands were found in the reviewed lines.

dangerous_patterns.title: Documented Shell Command Execution
dangerous_patterns.description: Markdown examples contain shell commands that an agent may execute when completing user workflows. Command execution should remain visible to users and limited to the documented Longbridge or package-install commands.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The shell examples are explicit and widespread. They are not malicious by themselves, but they are the main operational risk factor.

dangerous_patterns.title: Potential Sensitive Account Data Retrieval
dangerous_patterns.description: The hedging reference includes portfolio position commands that can require trade-scope authentication. This should only be used for user-requested portfolio analysis and should not be run as a default data-gathering step.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The sensitive-data concern is directly supported by the positions command and trade-scope note. There is no evidence of unauthorized transmission.

content.value_statement: Quant research often needs repeatable workflows for market data, factors, and strategy validation. This skill provides structured Longbridge CLI guidance and analysis templates for strategy research.
actual_capabilities: Guides Longbridge CLI use for K-line, factor, option, trade, and position data workflows.
Explains pairs trading, cointegration tests, hedge ratios, Z-scores, and half-life analysis.
Builds factor research workflows with IC, IR, decile testing, factor decay, and screening logic.
Supports volatility, seasonality, execution cost, hedging, and machine-learning strategy templates.
Provides multilingual output guidance for Simplified Chinese, Traditional Chinese, and English.
Includes error handling guidance for missing CLI tools, login failures, and insufficient data.
limitations: It depends on the Longbridge CLI, MCP tools, or Longbridge data being available in the environment.
It provides analysis workflows and examples, not personalized investment advice or trade execution.
Backtests and model outputs can be sensitive to data quality, lookback windows, and overfitting.
Some workflows may require authenticated Longbridge access, especially portfolio positions.

use_cases.title: Research a Pairs Trade
use_cases.description: Test two securities for cointegration, estimate the hedge ratio, and summarize spread signals.

use_cases.title: Screen a Factor Universe
use_cases.description: Rank an index or symbol list by valuation, quality, momentum, and low-volatility factors.

use_cases.title: Evaluate Hedging Costs
use_cases.description: Compare beta hedges, option protection, and tail-risk hedges using Longbridge market data.

prompt_templates.title: Basic Volatility Check
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner workflow for a single stock.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use Longbridge data to estimate the 20-day and 60-day historical volatility for TSLA.US. Explain the current volatility regime and include a risk note.

prompt_templates.title: Factor Screen
prompt_templates.scenario: Intermediate screening across a defined universe.
prompt_templates.prompt: Screen the CSI 300 universe for PE below 20, ROE above 12%, and dividend yield above 2%. Rank survivors by composite score and cite Longbridge as the data source.

prompt_templates.title: Pairs Trading Study
prompt_templates.prompt: Analyze AAPL.US and MSFT.US for a pairs trading setup. Fetch 252 daily candles, test cointegration, estimate the hedge ratio, and summarize the spread signal.

prompt_templates.title: Walk-Forward Strategy Review
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced strategy validation with overfitting controls.
prompt_templates.prompt: Build a walk-forward evaluation plan for a momentum strategy on NVDA.US. Include parameter ranges, in-sample and out-of-sample metrics, degradation checks, and caveats.

output_examples.input: Analyze whether two technology stocks are suitable for pairs trading.
output_examples.output: A concise report with data period, cointegration verdict, hedge ratio, spread Z-score, half-life, current signal, and risk caveats.

output_examples.input: Screen a stock universe using value and quality factors.
output_examples.output: A ranked table summary with passing symbols, factor scores, composite score, missing-data notes, and a methodology explanation.

output_examples.input: Estimate hedging choices for a concentrated equity position.
output_examples.output: A hedge objective summary, candidate instruments, cost versus protection comparison, scenario outcomes, and investment-advice disclaimer.

best_practices: Confirm Longbridge CLI flags with help commands before running data requests.
Use explicit symbols, dates, lookback windows, and assumptions in every analysis request.
Treat all outputs as research support and include data-source and investment-risk caveats.
anti_patterns: Do not treat a backtest, factor score, or model probability as a guaranteed trading signal.
Do not run authenticated portfolio commands unless the user clearly requested portfolio analysis.
Do not compare strategies without matching data windows, costs, and out-of-sample validation.

faq.question: Does this skill place trades?
faq.answer: No. It provides analysis workflows and data commands. It does not execute trades.

faq.question: Does it require a Longbridge login?
faq.answer: Market data workflows may be public, but portfolio position workflows can require authenticated Longbridge access.

faq.question: Can it generate Python analysis code?
faq.answer: Yes. Several references describe Python frameworks for statistics, backtesting, optimization, and machine learning.

faq.question: Can it analyze options strategies?
faq.answer: Yes. It covers volatility regimes, protective puts, collars, and other hedging workflows using options data.

faq.question: Is the output investment advice?
faq.answer: No. The skill repeatedly frames outputs as reference material and asks users to make independent decisions.

faq.question: Which languages does it support?
faq.answer: The skill asks the agent to match the user language in English, Simplified Chinese, or Traditional Chinese.
